title: The Printing Press
puzzle: Why did one machine change religion, politics, and learning all at once?
insight: The printing press made copying information dramatically cheaper and faster, which let ideas spread beyond the speed of handwriting.
key_ideas: Faster circulation changed literacy, debate, and reform.
mind_blower: The printing press multiplied not just books, but the speed of cultural change itself.
next_curiosity: If printing accelerated ideas, what technology later spread them even faster?
narration_text: Why did one machine change religion, politics, and learning all at once? The printing press made copying information dramatically cheaper and faster, which let ideas spread beyond the speed of handwriting. Movable type increased production speed. Printed material reached wider audiences. Faster circulation changed literacy, debate, and reform. The printing press multiplied not just books, but the speed of cultural change itself. If printing accelerated ideas, what technology later spread them even faster?
